title: Gladiator Giant Departs BBC Show After Three Seasons
summary: Jamie Bigg, known as Giant on the BBC show Gladiators, has announced his departure after three seasons. Bigg shared the news on Instagram, stating that being a Gladiator was one of the greatest honors of his life. However, he faced a choice that did not align with his values, leading to his exit. The show's spokesperson thanked him for his contributions and wished him well. Bigg, a former professional bodybuilder and firefighter from Ilkeston, stands 6ft 5in tall with 52cm biceps and consumes 10,000 calories daily to maintain his physique. He joined Gladiators when it returned in 2024. In January, he dropped his double-barrelled surname Christian-Johal after his divorce and ADHD diagnosis. Bigg emphasized that his departure was not his decision but a result of a values conflict. He expressed gratitude to fans and said this is a new beginning.

Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder, a condition affecting focus and behavior

background_read: Gladiators is a BBC show that features athletes competing in physical challenges. It returned in 2024 after a long hiatus. Jamie Bigg, known as Giant, was a standout Gladiator due to his size and strength. He was a professional bodybuilder and firefighter. His departure highlights the importance of personal values in career decisions.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Giant leaves Gladiators after three seasons due to a values conflict. Purpose: To inform readers about his departure and reasons. Evidence Evaluation: The article uses Giant's Instagram post and a show spokesperson's statement as primary sources. Author Credibility: The BBC is a reputable news source. Methodology: The information was gathered from social media and official statements.

perspectives.description: He values integrity and being a role model, leading him to leave when his values were challenged.
They respect his decision and thank him for his contributions.
Fans may be disappointed but supportive of his choice.
